mnemonics.title: main() function parts
mnemonics.content: Remember main() signature: 'I Return To OS' = int (return type), main, (parameters) - argc, argv

mnemonics.title: Stream operators
mnemonics.content: Think of 'cin >>' as 'cin takes in' (extraction), 'cout <<' as 'cout outputs' (insertion) - arrow points TOWARD the stream destination

mnemonics.title: Include syntax
mnemonics.content: System headers use < > (angle brackets look like pointed ears - system), user headers use " " (quotes for your own stuff)

acronyms.expansion: Argument Count - number of command-line arguments
acronyms.usage: Used in main(int argc, char* argv[]) to get number of arguments passed to program

acronyms.expansion: Argument Vector - array of character pointers
acronyms.usage: Used to access individual command-line arguments as strings

acronyms.expansion: Input Output Stream - provides I/O functionality
acronyms.usage: #include <iostream> to use cin, cout, cerr, clog

acronyms.expansion: Standard Template Library - collection of templates
acronyms.usage: Provides containers, algorithms, and iterators in C++

visualTips.visualization: Draw main as a box with 'entry point' label, showing argc/argv as inputs and return value (0 or 1) as output going to OS
Picture cin (keyboard) → (>>) → variables, and variables → (<<) → cout (screen) - arrows show data flow direction
Think of namespaces as folders. std::cout is like path: /std/cout. 'using namespace std;' is like 'cd std' to access contents directly

analogies.analogy: Like a library card catalog - they provide information about what's available (declarations) without containing the actual books (implementations)
Think of cout like a TV broadcast tower - data goes INTO it and gets transmitted TO the screen (viewers)
Like a microphone - sounds (data) go INTO it from the outside world and get processed INTO the program
argv is like a name tag array at a meeting - argv[0] is your own name tag, argv[1], argv[2] are other attendees' name tags
